summary: Spec 102 is a retirement spec (remove PreToolUse heuristic complete-matcher + harness prose path-extractor). The suite adequately pins both 'the removed thing is gone' and 'the preserved authoritative behavior still works'. R1: all 8 TestPanelGate_* pass fresh (SubThreshold_Blocks, git-merge-bead fence, recovery line, BlockNeverNamesSkipVar, SharedDecisionPin) — the in-binary gate is the authoritative enforcement and its coverage is unchanged and hermetic, fully standing in for the deleted slow LLM scenario (acknowledged behavioral-envelope loss is acceptable for a final gate). Setup migration is genuine: TestRunClaude_FreshSetup, TestWantedHooks_NoPreCompletePreToolUse, and TestRunClaude_CleansStalePreToolUse all flipped from assert-present to assert-ABSENT, and the entry is removed-by-omission via removeStaleMindspecEntries (spec-072 precedent). The wantedWithPreComplete() merge-machinery tests (Landmine A/B, user-survival, idempotence) correctly inject a SYNTHETIC wanted set to keep merge plumbing coverage alive, decoupled from the real wantedHooks() which no longer ships the entry — no contradiction. TestNames_Sorted passes with pre-complete dropped. R2: TestAnalyzer_PlanFidelity_PathTermRetired is a GENUINE exact-score RED pin — verified by static trace against the saved main analyzer.go: old total=len(paths)+len(cmds)=2, path matched -> 1/2=0.5; new commands-only total=1, no command matched -> 0/1=0.0. The test asserts EXACTLY 0.0, so it fails against pre-change code (0.5) and passes after the path-term removal; not vacuous. High/Low locks still pass commands-only (go test mentioned + run -> high; unrelated -> low). HERMETIC: changed tests (analyzer_test.go, claude_test.go) are pure units with zero LLM-scenario dependency; filtered harness run completes in <1s; no go test ./internal/harness/... required. golangci-lint run over the four changed-package globs reports 0 issues (the CI lint gate that caught spec 101). go build ./... and go vet ./internal/harness/ clean; no dangling references to ScenarioPanelGateBlocksPrematureComplete, extractMentionedPaths, expectedPaths, or touchedPaths. No critical behavior left unpinned by the removal.
